exemplar.title: How does pre-performance static stretching compared to dynamic stretches (IV) impact the hand-eye coordination of a male cricket player, aged 16-18, measured by the number of successful catches across a 30 second (+/- 0.25 seconds) time period in an alternate hand-wall toss (DV)?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

detailedExemplarData.title: How does pre-performance static stretching compared to dynamic stretches (IV) impact the hand-eye coordination of a male cricket player, aged 16-18, measured by the number of successful catches across a 30 second (+/- 0.25 seconds) time period in an alternate hand-wall toss (DV)?

subject.title: Sports, exercise and health science (SEHS - Old)

strengths: Vivid cultural connection to cricket and seven-year club experience demonstrating clear personal engagement
Investigation choice and design driven by studentâs own performance goals, showing sustained independent thinking throughout
weaknesses: Limited reflection on how the findings inform the studentâs broader personal or academic growth
Could include more personal narrative on challenges encountered during data collection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs rationale and design are explicitly linked to his personal experience in cricket, with sustained initiative in selecting, implementing, and justifying the investigation. This fully meets the top-band descriptors for personal engagement (clear significance, independent thought and initiative).

strengths: Research question is fully focused, specifying IV, DV, population, and measurement method
Comprehensive safety and ethical considerations (detailed PAR-Q, environmental and consent controls)
Extensive control list demonstrating high methodological planning
Background material is mainly appropriate, linking literature findings to the investigationâs hypothesis
weaknesses: Method does not include counterbalancing of static vs dynamic conditions or justification of timing error margins
Background relies in part on non-peer-reviewed sources and speculative application of boxing studies to cricket
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The study presents a highly relevant and focused research question and shows full awareness of safety and ethical issues. The methodology is mainly appropriate (with clear control of confounding factors) and the background is largely relevant. While some limitations exist (method counterbalancing, source rigor), the overall exploration performance merits a score above mid-band, justifying a 5 to reflect the investigationâs strong focus and planning.

strengths: Raw data are comprehensive (270 data points) with clear units and no missing entries
Statistical processing (means, standard deviations, t-test) is appropriate and largely accurate
Interpretation is broadly valid and detailed, with plausible explanations for anomalies
weaknesses: Consideration of measurement uncertainty is limited (no propagation calculations or confidence intervals)
Some inconsistencies in rounding and lack of formal justification for choice of unpaired vs paired t-test
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis demonstrates sufficient, relevant raw data and appropriate processing, with correct and detailed interpretation. However, treatment of uncertainty is only partial and occasional processing inconsistencies prevent placement in the top band, aligning with a solid mid-band score of 4.

strengths: Conclusion directly addresses the research question and is supported by statistical findings
Relevant comparisons to multiple scientific studies are correctly justified
Methodological limitations are clearly identified and their effects on data quality are well discussed
weaknesses: Suggestions for improvement are realistic but lack detailed implementation strategies (e.g., power analysis, recruitment matching)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation includes a well-supported conclusion, justified comparison to context, and clear discussion of limitations, satisfying the upper mid-band descriptors. Suggestions, while relevant, are not sufficiently detailed to reach the top band, resulting in a score of 4.

strengths: Report is well structured with logical IB-style sections and appropriately labeled figures
Use of subject-specific terminology is correct with only minor errors
weaknesses: Presentation impeded by typographical artifacts and dense paragraphs reducing clarity
Inconsistencies in table/graph numbering, caption placement, and unit notation affect conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the communication is clear and follows a coherent structure, meeting the 3â4 band. However, frequent formatting and clarity issues, along with lapses in conciseness and consistency, prevent awarding the top mark in this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: Instrument precision (Â±0.25 s, Â±0.25 cm) is cited and repeated in the text, yet there is no propagation, no discussion of percentage error, and no comment on biological variability.  This demonstrates **some** consideration but not full treatment, warranting a mid-band score of 3.

The student relates findings to several external studies on stretching and coordination, accurately identifying agreements and divergences.  While depth is moderate, the comparison is **relevant and justified**, meeting the upper 3-4 band.

Sections (Introduction, Method, Results, Evaluation, Bibliography) are present in logical order; headings, bullet points and tables aid navigation.  No major component is missing, satisfying the top band for structure (4).

Suggestions (increase sample, extend stretch time, standardise BMI/fitness, indoor testing) are realistic and connected to stated limitations but lack detail on implementation or expected impact magnitude.  Hence a competent but not exhaustive treatment â score 3.

The research question is concise, identifies IV, DV, population and measurement method, and is revisited verbatim throughout.  It is therefore both relevant and fully focused.  Minor repetition of wording does not detract from focus, so a highâband (5) award is justified.

Four concrete limitations (sample size, stretch duration, participant heterogeneity, weather) are analysed with explanation of their effect on data quality.  Discussion shows sound methodological understanding, meriting 4 in the 3-4 band.

Method covers participants, equipment, step-by-step procedures and extensive control list, but weaknesses persist: sample size is small, static stretch is a plank (strength not stretch), timing error margins not justified, and no counter-balancing order of conditions.  These points show the design is *mainly* appropriate with clear limitations, fitting the mid-band score of 3.

A short literature review summarises five external studies, defining static/dynamic stretching and linking previous findings to the hypothesis.  Citations are present, yet some sources are popular-science websites and links between balance/agility papers and hand-eye coordination are speculative.  Consequently the background is **mainly** (not entirely) appropriate and relevant, meriting the upper end of the 3â4 band.

Most content relates directly to answering the RQ; nevertheless extended quotations of PAR-Q forms and very long literature descriptions reduce conciseness.  The investigation therefore remains largely focused but not consistently concise â score 3.

Interpretation links numerical trends, SD, t-test and literature; anomalies are explained with plausible environmental factors.  Nonetheless, claims about neural communication and causality are occasionally overstated.  Overall the interpretation is **broadly correct and detailed**, deserving 4.

The report follows a logical IB-style structure and most tables/figures are labelled, so clarity is generally good.  However, typographical artefacts (e.g., stray symbols in tables) and long, dense paragraphs impede fluency, so a solid but not perfect presentation (score 3).

The student repeatedly links the investigation to his seven-year personal involvement in club cricket and his cultural connection to the sport.  He selects a skill (hand-eye coordination) that he personally wishes to improve and designs a study that he can perform with club team-mates, demonstrating clear independent thought (choice of specific stretching modes, own data collection).  These examples represent **sustained and explicit personal engagement** rather than occasional references.

Processing includes means, a clearly-labelled graph, manual SD example and an unpaired t-test with p-value.  Calculations appear correct but the SD work shows rounding inconsistencies and the t-test assumptions (paired vs unpaired) are not justified.  This is **appropriate but occasionally inconsistent** processing, placing it at the top of the 3-4 band (4).

The rationale explains why hand-eye coordination limits the studentâs fielding performance and why identifying the more effective warm-up would be valuable to him as a player.  The justification is specific (optimising pre-performance routine in cricket) and conveys genuine curiosity, fully satisfying the descriptor for clear personal significance.

Three full raw-data tables record 30 trials per condition (270 data points).  Values are sensible, units/time frame stated and no missing entries.  This constitutes **sufficient, relevant raw data** supporting later analysis, meeting the 5â6 descriptor (score 5).

The investigation was student-driven: he chose a feasible wall-toss protocol, constructed detailed control lists, produced his own PAR-Q for ethics, and collected / processed all primary data.  Such decisions display **initiative across design, implementation and presentation**, matching the top band.

The student addresses safety (PAR-Q screening, lighting, obstacles) and ethics (consent, anonymity, noise disturbance).  All significant issues for adolescent physical testing are identified, demonstrating **full awareness**; hence a high-band score of 5 is appropriate.

Sport-science terminology (IV, DV, standard deviation, t-test) is used correctly and referencing is present.  Minor misuse (âplankâ classed as static stretch) and occasional formatting errors occur, so usage is **appropriate with some errors**, matching a score of 3.

The conclusion restates the RQ, summarises data and statistical findings and is aligned with evidence.  Support is adequate but tempered by small sample size discussion, fitting the 3-4 band (score 4).

annotations.comment: The research question explicitly identifies the independent variable, dependent variable, population, and measurement, demonstrating strong focus and relevance.
Re-stating the research question verbatim is redundant; consider removing the repetition here to maintain conciseness.
The hypothesis is clear but would be stronger if you explicitly stated both null and alternative hypotheses for statistical testing.
The controlled variables section is exceptionally thorough, demonstrating clear methodological planning to minimize confounding factors.
The age control description is overly verbose; condense the rationale to improve readability and avoid redundancy.
No counterbalancing of trial order is described; randomizing the sequence of static and dynamic conditions would reduce order effects.
The confounding variables list is well-constructed, evidencing awareness of environmental and personal factors beyond control.
The method for static and dynamic exercise types should clearly define both exercises; currently only planks are specified for static stretches.
The equipment list specifies â130g Â± 0.15 mg,â which is inconsistent; change âmgâ to âgâ to accurately reflect mass precision.
Figure 1 clearly illustrates the alternate hand-wall toss, supporting readersâ understanding of the procedure.
The studentâs vivid cultural connection to cricket and 7-year club experience enhances personal engagement and shows independent thinking throughout the investigation.
The phrase âhand-eve coordinationâ is a typographical error; please correct it to âhand-eye coordinationâ to maintain professionalism.
Background relies on non-peer-reviewed sites and speculative links; incorporate more sport-science journal sources and strengthen justification of relevance.
Definitions of static and dynamic stretching are adequate, but dependence on general health websites reduces academic rigor; consider peer-reviewed sport-science sources.
The Ellison Paul study is relevant, but its direct application to pre-performance stretching should be elaborated to better link it to your research question.
While noting the boxing studyâs limitation, discuss how differences in skill demands between boxing and cricket may affect hand-eye coordination outcomes.
The neuroscience discussion is thorough but dense; breaking into shorter sentences or bullet points would enhance clarity and reader engagement.
The discussion of wall material elasticity is informative but could be shortened to focus on its direct impact on rebound speed.
Procedure lacks random assignment of stretch conditions; counterbalancing the order would reduce potential sequence bias.
Raw data in Table 1 is comprehensive and supports detailed analysis, meeting high standards for data quality.
Inconsistent mathematical symbols (Ï, overline) and missing standard notation in Table 1 may confuse readers; standardize notation throughout.
Interpreting SD in terms of variability is correct; consider discussing percentage error or confidence intervals to deepen uncertainty treatment.
Highlighting the use of a no-stretch baseline is a major strength, providing a clear reference for evaluating both static and dynamic stretch effects.
Limitations are well identified, but future improvement suggestions lack implementation details; specify recruitment strategy, power analysis or protocol adjustments.
Discussing the small sample size is appropriate; conducting a statistical power analysis would strengthen justification for future participant numbers.
The recommendation to standardize participant characteristics is realistic; including specific recruitment criteria or matching procedures would enhance reliability.
In-depth discussion of weather and environmental factors shows strong methodological awareness of uncontrollable influences on data quality.
The caption for Table 1 appears below the table; placing captions above tables is standard practice to guide interpretation.
Include units alongside average catch rates in Tables 2 and 3 (e.g., catches per 30 s) to improve clarity for the reader.
Participant 7âs average of 11 catches under dynamic stretching is a clear outlier; discuss its impact or consider robust measures to address it.
Graph and table numbering are inconsistent across sections; aligning labels (Table 2, Table 3, Graph 1) will improve organization and navigation.
Graph 1 would benefit from error bars representing standard deviation to visually convey variability alongside the means.
The standard deviation formula lacks explicit definitions of symbols (e.g., clarify s, n, Î£); define each term for accuracy.
The T-test description should specify degrees of freedom and justify whether a paired or unpaired test was used to validate appropriateness.
Describe the statistical test software and steps taken (e.g., paired t-test on raw means) to improve transparency of analysis.
The conclusion introduces qualitative data (player/coach interviews) without outlining the qualitative methodology; include method details or separate qualitative findings.
The conclusion is overly verbose; summarizing key findings succinctly will maintain focus and enhance readability.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Justification shows little personal significance or curiosity
Little evidence of personal input in design, implementation or presentation
Limited evidence of personal engagement with little independent thinking
Clear evidence of personal input and initiative throughout the investigation
Clear evidence of personal engagement with significant independent thinking
Justification demonstrates clear personal significance and curiosity

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Evidence of personal engagement and independent thinking

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: Superficial or limited relevance background information
Research question stated but not focused
Methodology appropriate to very limited extent
Mainly appropriate and relevant background information
Relevant but not fully focused research question
Entirely appropriate and relevant background information
Highly appropriate methodology addressing all significant factors
Relevant and fully focused research question
Sufficient relevant raw data supporting detailed conclusion
Basic outline of practical issues only
Some relevant comparison to scientific context
Detailed, relevant and fully supported conclusion
Clear understanding of methodological issues demonstrated
Correct and justified comparison to scientific context
Many errors in terminology and conventions
Appropriate use of terminology with minimal errors
Well structured with all necessary information
